message:
🐛 Fix operator cache poisoning and add retry for transient kubectl errors (#982)

Two bugs caused "0 operators" on cold start:

1. Cache poisoning: When kubectl get csv fails (HTTP/2 stream errors,
   connection resets), the empty result was cached for 5 minutes. This
   prevented any retry until TTL expiry, showing 0 operators even on
   page refresh.

2. No retry: Large OpenShift clusters (1000+ CSVs, 191MB JSON) often
   trigger HTTP/2 INTERNAL_ERROR on first attempt due to API server
   connection pressure during dashboard startup thundering herd.

Fixes:
- Change fetchOperatorsFromCluster to return ([]Operator, error)
- Only cache successful results (empty or not) — never cache errors
- Add single retry with 2s backoff for transient kubectl failures
- Add --request-timeout=0 to kubectl to let our context timeout
  handle cancellation instead of kubectl's default 0s behavior

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
